summary: Gruber reveals that the iPhone 11 Pro's telephoto camera does not actually support Night Mode, contrary to widespread assumption. When shooting at 2× zoom in Night Mode, the phone silently switches to the wide-angle camera and uses digital zoom instead of the optical telephoto lens. This was confirmed by Halide co-creator Sebastiaan de With and can be verified through EXIF data or by physically covering individual lenses. Gruber speculates that Apple has been deliberately vague about this limitation because it highlights that the cheaper iPhone 11 can produce identical 2× Night Mode shots as the iPhone 11 Pro, since both phones rely on the same wide-angle sensor for these images.
key_points: Night Mode on iPhone 11 Pro only works with the wide-angle camera, not the telephoto or ultra-wide lenses
When shooting 2× Night Mode, the phone uses digital zoom from the wide-angle sensor rather than the telephoto camera — a behavior verifiable through EXIF data
Previous dual-camera iPhones similarly fell back to digital zoom in low light, but Night Mode made the wrong assumption more widespread
Apple has been deliberately unclear about this limitation, likely because it reveals the iPhone 11 matches the 11 Pro for 2× Night Mode shots
Deep Fusion, coming in iOS 13.2, will add another layer of automatic camera mode selection based on light levels and lens
money_quotes: Cover the telephoto and you can still shoot '2×' Night Mode shots; cover the regular wide lens and you can't.
2× doesn't mean you're always using the telephoto camera — in low light it will use the wide-angle camera and digital zoom.

It occurs to me that this is why Apple has been somewhat obfuscatory about Night Mode working only with the regular wide angle camera, despite being very forthcoming about explaining other technical details (like Deep Fusion) at great length.
It's yet another way that the iPhone 11 is the technical equal to the significantly more expensive 11 Pro.
There's nothing scandalous about this — everyone loves Night Mode, including for 2× field-of-view photos.
key_insight: Apple's silence about Night Mode being limited to the wide-angle camera quietly obscures the fact that the cheaper iPhone 11 produces identical 2× Night Mode photos as the iPhone 11 Pro.
